title: bug: podman passes empty XDG_ variable to OCI runtime
body:
/kind bug


**Description**

Using runsc without adding `--runtime-flag root=/var/lib/containers` in podman causes runsc to complain that it cannot find the container

Because of the cgroup issue in runsc release-20211019.0, release-20211011.0 is used here

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sudo podman --cgroup-manager cgroupfs --runtime /bin/runsc --runtime-flag debug --runtime-flag debug-log=/tmp/runsc run --rm hello-world`

**Describe the results you received:**

/tmp/runsc: https://fars.ee/u__p
podman output:
```
WARN[0000] Failed to add conmon to cgroupfs sandbox cgroup: error creating cgroup path for net_cls: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/net_cls/libpod_parent: read-only file system 
loading container: file does not exist
Error: `/bin/runsc --debug --debug-log=/tmp/runsc start d8a7a9929c126fa4b23b63716aedaa37937a50485270b91a4efd31c3c9540a19` failed: exit status 128
```
**Describe the results you expected:**

Normal operation of the container

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

https://github.com/google/gvisor/issues/5887

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.1
API Version:  3.4.1
Go Version:   go1.17.1
Git Commit:   a6493ae6904a5b7dde2395a374cb77fc45c8a3bc
Built:        Thu Oct 21 01:27:47 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: How is this a Podman bug and how do you suggest we fix it?

> How is this a Podman bug and how do you suggest we fix it?

How do other OCI runtimes handle this issue?
OCI Runtimes do not need to deal with container storage, they deal with already mounted images on rootfs. Podman and container engines manage the storage.

> OCI Runtimes do not need to deal with container storage, they deal with already mounted images on rootfs. Podman and container engines manage the storage.

offtopic: Can you create a telegram group for general questions and discussion?

> OCI Runtimes do not need to deal with container storage, they deal with already mounted images on rootfs. Podman and container engines manage the storage.

Is the --root flag that runsc wants the location of the rootfs?

try:
```
sudo -E -i  podman --cgroup-manager cgroupfs --runtime /bin/runsc --runtime-flag debug --runtime-flag debug-log=/tmp/runsc run --rm hello-world
```

> try:
> 
> ```
> sudo -E -i  podman --cgroup-manager cgroupfs --runtime /bin/runsc --runtime-flag debug --runtime-flag debug-log=/tmp/runsc run --rm hello-world
> ```

`sudo: you may not specify both the -i and -E options`
One more thing, the contents of `/tmp/runsc` are already in the description

I think the same for the crun case, but in the special case: 1. Fedora 34, i.e BTRFS storage driver. 2. Podman invoked from the Systemd unit, i.e. storage root and Cgroup structure are not pre-defined by User's session. The result is "invalid reference" when binding volume. I tried to pass everything from the systemd environment but something is still missing. It can't be configured using crun configuration, only passed like in the FUSE case.

For curiosity only: try
sudo systemctl --user xxxx
Failed to connect to bus: $DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS and $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR not defined (consider using --machine=<user>@.host --user to connect to bus of other user)
to see what is needed, at least, to make systemd as cgroup manager working.

The crun issue is almost certainly unrelated.

For this issue, I think the actual solution is a new field in `containers.conf` to specify per-runtime arguments that should be added by default - see https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/91.

@mheon Related. ICrun needs many things that exported to the session environment by the services managed by the systemd --user. From the systemd point-of-view it is easy: PassEnvironment ...
On the Desktops Terminal is a plain Gnome GUI application reusing Session environment generated by systemd, exported to the session.service, like XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, Logger socket path, DBUS socket path. Journald socket exprted by the corresponding socket unit, tty init, ...
The same is true for CG tree.
To see the actual environment and  CG env is unusable. The real, up-to-moment picture is visible thrue systemd-cgls and systemctl --user show-environment.
Even to pass something from the /proc/self/environ to the container process inside container scope created by Crun Podman has to know what to pass and how to pass. Config is nice for sdanalone processes by it has to be boxed inside rootless GUI session, $XDG_CONFIG_HOME for example.
CRun CLI itself sufers from the same issue - it doesn't work correctly in the Linux Desktop terminal: the "static" Cgroup presence and API is not reliable because the real pre-requisits are 1. User session has started, i.e, $HOME, $XDG etc are exported to the environment and D-Bus broker has started, ready to accept "Create scope" message to the correct socket. 
If Podman can't work on Linux Desktops and WSL with systemd and GUI and always needs Terminal server, so what is its benefit?

https://fars.ee/KhKd

This is the output of /tmp/runsc when docker uses runsc

@rhatdan I want to know what environment variables podman passes to oci runtime

Related questions: https://github.com/google/gvisor/issues/6849#issuecomment-966525492
It has since been fixed in runsc to not use an empty XDG_ variable, so I am closing the issue.
If I had time, I would try to fix it

> 

@PavelSosin-320 The problem remains the same now?
